points.text: Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 trial met primary endpoint with 87% reduction in swelling attacks vs placebo over six months; positions company to seek approval for first in vivo CRISPR gene editing medicine.
Intellia Therapeutics announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 HAELO trial for lonvo-z, marking the first reported Phase 3 data for an in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy.
Intellia Therapeutics initiated a rolling BLA submission to the FDA for lonvo-z, an in vivo CRISPR candidate targeting the KLKB1 gene for hereditary angioedema.
Intellia Therapeutics is preparing for a potential U.S. launch of lonvo-z in the first half of 2027 following successful Phase 3 CRISPR gene editing trial outcomes.
Intellia Therapeutics priced a public offering of 16,744,187 shares at $10.75 per share, raising approximately $180 million in gross proceeds to advance its CRISPR gene editing pipeline.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Public Offering — Announced pricing of public offering of common stock on April 29, 2026, to fund CRISPR-based therapeutic development.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Workforce Expansion — Awarded inducement grants of 208,850 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 43 new employees on May 1, 2026, under the 2024 Inducement Plan.
ElevateBio presented nine abstracts at the ASGCT 29th Annual Meeting showcasing advancements in gene editing, including specific optimization of CRISPR systems for adenosine base editor activity.

Editas Medicine secured a third favorable U.S. Patent and Trademark Office decision affirming Broad Institute's priority for CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing in eukaryotic cells, reinforcing IP for EDIT-401 development.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics' exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) utilizes CRISPR-Cas9 to edit the BCL11A gene for fetal hemoglobin production in sickle cell disease patients aged 12+.
Eli Lilly entered a strategic collaboration with AI-focused biotech Profluent to develop next-generation gene editors capable of inserting entire genes into patients.
Two NEJM studies confirmed functional cure potential for sickle cell disease using CRISPR-Cas9 (Casgevy) and base editing (Beam Therapeutics) to induce fetal hemoglobin expression.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 trial succeeded; 87% reduction in attack rate vs placebo; 62% of recipients achieved complete freedom from attacks or need for rescue medication.

Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial met primary endpoints in 80 patients (52 treated, 28 placebo) with a one-time 50 mg in vivo CRISPR dose inactivating KLKB1; rolling BLA submitted to FDA.
Intellia Therapeutics — Common Stock Offering — Capital Raise — Priced 16,744,187 shares at $10.75/share for ~$180M gross proceeds; underwriters hold 30-day option for 2,511,628 additional shares.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR platform — Capital Markets — Completed pricing of public offering of common stock on April 29, 2026, to support clinical-stage CRISPR gene editing programs.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Talent Acquisition — Granted 208,850 time-based RSUs vesting annually over three years to 43 new hires as material inducement under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).
ElevateBio — CRISPR systems — Monogenic disease correction — Presented poster #1303 detailing protein and gRNA engineering of diverse CRISPR systems to optimize adenosine base editor activity.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 Patent Interference — Intellectual Property — U.S. PTAB reaffirmed Broad Institute's inventorship priority for CRISPR/Cas9 in human cells, marking third favorable decision against CVC; CVC retains right to appeal to CAFC.

Intellia Therapeutics — Inducement Grants — Corporate Governance — Awarded 208,850 time-based RSUs to 43 new hires under 2024 Inducement Plan; one-third vest annually over three years to support CRISPR-based therapeutic development.

Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 trial met primary endpoint with 87% reduction in swelling attacks vs placebo over 6 months; 62% of recipients achieved attack-free status.

CRISPR Therapeutics / Vertex Pharmaceuticals — exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) — Sickle Cell Disease — FDA-approved CRISPR-Cas9 therapy targeting BCL11A gene to induce fetal hemoglobin; indicated for patients aged 12+ with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises.
Editas Medicine — EDIT-401 — Hyperlipidemia — Preclinical data presented at scientific conferences showing >90% mean LDL cholesterol reduction in non-human primates via CRISPR/Cas9 mechanism.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 Platform — Intellectual Property — USPTO reaffirmed Broad Institute's inventorship priority for CRISPR/Cas9 in eukaryotic cells, reinforcing Editas' exclusive license.
ElevateBio — CRISPR Systems — Gene Editing Services — Presented poster (#1303) on leveraging protein and gRNA engineering of diverse CRISPR systems to optimize adenosine base editor activity.
Intellia Therapeutics — Equity Incentive — CRISPR Gene Editing Platform — Granted 208,850 RSUs to 43 new hires under 2024 Inducement Plan; vesting over 3 years to retain talent for clinical programs.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals / CRISPR Therapeutics — Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Sickle Cell Disease — NEJM study confirmed CRISPR-Cas9 BCL11A editing led to normalization of hemoglobin and durable fetal hemoglobin expression.
Beam Therapeutics — Ristoglogene autogetemcel — Sickle Cell Disease — NEJM study demonstrated rapid engraftment and durable fetal hemoglobin expression via base editing mechanism.

Eli Lilly — Profluent collaboration — Gene editing (CRISPR) — Agreement to develop new gene editors with potential for whole-gene insertion; up to $2.25 billion in milestone payments if all programs succeed.
Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 trial: 87% reduction in attack rates; >60% of patients attack-free; no serious safety signals reported.

Eli Lilly — Profluent collaboration — Gene editing (CRISPR-associated) — Deal includes potential $2.25B in milestone payments to develop gene editors for inserting entire genes; specific programs and upfront costs undisclosed.
Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — One-time CRISPR therapy demonstrated 87% attack rate reduction in Phase 3 trial; positioned as functional cure candidate.
Intellia Therapeutics — Commercialization of first in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy — Lonvo-z — Phase 3 data positive; regulatory submission anticipated following April 2026 trial success.
Intellia Therapeutics — Commercialization of first-in-class in vivo CRISPR therapy — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Phase 3 HAELO trial completed; rolling BLA submitted; U.S. launch targeted H1 2027.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization and pipeline advancement — NTLA common stock — Secured $180M capital via public offering to fund CRISPR-based therapeutic programs.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — Clinical-stage pipeline — Secured capital via public offering and expanded workforce with 43 inducement grants to advance curative CRISPR treatments.

ElevateBio — Gene editing services and CDMO — CRISPR platform + LNP delivery — Expanding toolbox beyond nuclease editing to include large gene insertion and epigenetic editing; presenting 9 abstracts at ASGCT 2026.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 IP protection and in vivo gene editing commercialization — EDIT-401 (hyperlipidemia) — PTAB decision reinforces foundational CRISPR/Cas9 patents; preclinical data shows >90% mean LDL reduction in non-human primates.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing platform scaling — NTLA pipeline — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees; company remains clinical-stage biopharmaceutical focused on curative CRISPR treatments.
Intellia Therapeutics — In vivo CRISPR commercialization — lonvo-z — Phase 3 success in hereditary angioedema; positioned to launch first in vivo gene editing medicine.

CRISPR Therapeutics / Vertex Pharmaceuticals — Ex vivo CRISPR therapy scale-up — exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) — FDA approved for sickle cell disease; manufacturing and access challenges noted despite functional cure potential.

Intellia Therapeutics — Commercialization of first-in-class in vivo CRISPR therapy — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Phase 3 HAELO data positive; BLA rolling submission initiated; $180M capital raise secured April 2026.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR-based curative treatment development — Clinical-stage pipeline — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees to accelerate CRISPR medicine revolution; grants approved by compensation committee under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).

Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 therapeutic development — EDIT-401 — Secured foundational IP rights following USPTO decision; advancing one-time in vivo therapy for hyperlipidemia with >90% LDL reduction data.
ElevateBio — CRISPR and gene editing CDMO services — CRISPR systems optimization — Expanding toolbox with protein/gRNA engineering strategies to enhance base editor activity for monogenic diseases.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR clinical development and talent acquisition — NTLA pipeline — Awarded 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees to accelerate CRISPR-based therapeutic programs.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals / CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercialization of curative gene therapies — Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) — FDA-approved for SCD; NEJM data confirms functional cure via BCL11A disruption.
Beam Therapeutics — Base editing platform advancement — Ristoglogene autogetemcel — NEJM data shows durable fetal hemoglobin expression in SCD patients; positioned as alternative to CRISPR-Cas9.

Eli Lilly — Expansion into genetic medicine — CRISPR-based gene editing programs via Profluent partnership — Established new genetic medicine center in Boston; acquired multiple gene therapy/editing firms in recent years.

Profluent — AI-driven gene editor development — Next-generation CRISPR platforms — Partnered with Eli Lilly to create editors capable of whole-gene insertion; specific program count and upfront payment undisclosed.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR therapy commercialization — lonvo-z (NTLA-2001) — Phase 3 data supports functional cure positioning for hereditary angioedema; 87% attack rate reduction achieved.

Eli Lilly — Expansion into genetic medicine — CRISPR/gene editing pipeline via Profluent partnership — Leveraging obesity drug revenues to fund new Boston genetic medicine center and acquire gene therapy assets.

Profluent — AI-driven gene editor development — Next-generation CRISPR systems — Partnered with Eli Lilly to create editors capable of whole-gene insertion; financial terms include up to $2.25B in milestones.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR-based gene editing commercialization — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Phase 3 data supports functional cure potential for hereditary angioedema with 87% attack reduction.

Lonvo-z — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing — Phase: 3 — Population: Hereditary Angioedema patients — Comparator: Placebo — Results: 87% reduction in swelling attacks; 62% of patients achieved attack-free status or no rescue medication need over six months.

lonvo-z — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing to inactivate kallikrein B1 (KLKB1) gene — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Adults and adolescents (≥16 years) with Type I or II HAE — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints: Reduction in HAE attack frequency, quality of life, safety; 50 mg single dose administered.

Adenosine base editor — Mechanism: CRISPR-mediated protein and gRNA engineering — Phase: Preclinical/Discovery — Population: Monogenic disease models — Results: Optimization strategies presented for correcting causative mutations via poster #1303.

EDIT-401 — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 in vivo gene editing targeting PCSK9 — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Non-human primates — Endpoints/Results: Achieved >90% mean LDL cholesterol reduction; potential best-in-class one-time therapy for hyperlipidemia.

lonvo-z — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR base editing — Phase: 3 — Population: Hereditary angioedema patients — Comparator: Placebo — Results: 87% reduction in swelling attack rate; 62% of patients achieved attack-free status or eliminated need for rescue therapy.

exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) — Mechanism: Ex vivo CRISPR-Cas9 editing of BCL11A enhancer — Phase: Approved (Post-marketing) — Population: Sickle cell disease patients aged 12+ — Comparator: N/A (Standard of care) — Results: Normalization of total hemoglobin and sustained fetal hemoglobin expression.

lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing to inactivate KLKB1 gene, reducing kallikrein and bradykinin levels — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Adults/adolescents (≥16y) with Type I/II HAE — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints: Reduction in HAE attack frequency, quality of life, safety; 50mg one-time dose administered outpatient.
EDIT-401 — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 in vivo gene editing — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Non-human primates — Comparator: Baseline — Endpoints/Results: >90% mean LDL cholesterol reduction achieved.

Adenosine Base Editors — Mechanism: CRISPR system engineering (protein and gRNA optimization) — Phase: Preclinical research — Population: Monogenic disease models — Endpoints/Results: Enhanced activity for causative mutation correction presented at ASGCT 2026.

Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) — Mechanism: CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of BCL11A erythroid enhancer — Phase: Approved/Post-marketing — Population: Sickle cell disease patients ≥12 years — Comparator: Historical controls — Results: Normalization of total hemoglobin and sustained fetal hemoglobin elevation.

Ristoglogene autogetemcel — Mechanism: Base editing to upregulate fetal hemoglobin — Phase: Clinical — Population: Sickle cell disease patients — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Rapid engraftment and durable fetal hemoglobin expression reported in NEJM study.

Profluent/Eli Lilly joint programs — Mechanism: AI-designed CRISPR gene editors for whole-gene insertion — Phase: Preclinical/early development — Population: Undisclosed disease targets — Endpoints: Development of novel editors capable of inserting entire genes.

lonvo-z — Mechanism: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing targeting TTR gene — Phase: 3 — Population: Hereditary angioedema patients — Comparator: Historical controls (implied by attack rate reduction) — Results: 87% attack rate reduction; >60% patients attack-free; no serious safety signals.

Profluent/Eli Lilly joint program — Mechanism: AI-designed gene editors for whole-gene insertion (CRISPR-associated) — Phase: Preclinical/Early development — Population: Not specified — Comparator: N/A — Status: Collaboration announced; specific disease targets undisclosed.

lonvo-z — Mechanism: CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing targeting TTR gene — Phase: 3 — Population: Hereditary angioedema patients — Comparator: Historical controls — Results: 87% attack rate reduction; >60% attack-free; no serious safety signals.

CRISPR-Cas9 — Mechanism: RNA-guided DNA endonuclease gene editing — Phase: Preclinical/Research — Population: General cellular reprogramming contexts — Comparator: None specified — Results: Cited as a transformative milestone enabling cellular reprogramming strategies alongside iPSCs.
Intellia Therapeutics — Strategic positioning for first-in-class CRISPR commercialization — Lonvo-z — Investor debate continues regarding commercial potential despite late-stage clinical success.
Intellia Therapeutics — Regulatory filing strategy — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Initiated rolling BLA submission to FDA for in vivo CRISPR therapy; potential U.S. launch scheduled for first half of 2027.

Intellia Therapeutics — Equity financing — CRISPR pipeline funding — Completed underwritten public offering of 16,744,187 shares at $10.75/share; Jefferies, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup acting as joint book-running managers.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Equity Financing — Announced pricing of public offering of common stock on April 29, 2026, to fund operations and CRISPR therapeutic development.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing — Equity Compensation — Issued 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees under the 2024 Inducement Plan, with one-third vesting annually over three years.

Editas Medicine — Intellectual Property litigation outcome — CRISPR/Cas9 patents — U.S. PTAB reaffirmed Broad Institute's priority in interference vs CVC; foundational CRISPR/Cas9 and CRISPR/Cas12a licenses remain unaffected and exclusive to Editas.

Intellia Therapeutics — Equity compensation expansion — CRISPR pipeline staffing — Granted 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees under 2024 Inducement Plan; approved by compensation committee per Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).
Intellia Therapeutics — Commercialization strategy for first in vivo CRISPR drug — lonvo-z — Market potential debated by investors following Phase 3 success in hereditary angioedema.

Intellia Therapeutics — Public Offering — Common Stock — Priced 16,744,187 shares at $10.75/share; gross proceeds ~$180M; 30-day over-allotment option for 2.5M shares; joint book-runners: Jefferies, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup.

Intellia Therapeutics — Equity compensation strategy — 2024 Inducement Plan — Issued 208,850 RSUs to 43 new employees as material inducement for employment; grants vest over three years subject to continued service.

Editas Medicine — Intellectual Property Portfolio — CRISPR/Cas9 — USPTO decision reaffirms Broad Institute priority, securing exclusive license for CRISPR/Cas9 in human cells; CVC retains right to appeal.
Intellia Therapeutics — Equity compensation strategy — CRISPR pipeline support — Issued 208,850 RSUs under 2024 Inducement Plan; approved by compensation committee under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).
Eli Lilly / Profluent — Strategic collaboration agreement — CRISPR gene editing — Potential $2.25 billion in milestone payments; upfront payment and specific disease indications undisclosed.
Eli Lilly / Profluent — Strategic collaboration — Gene editing platform — Potential $2.25B in milestone payments; upfront payment and number of programs undisclosed.
